issues: color_temperature_mismatch: candidate introduces cold bright blue (flame/crystal head) absent from all peer warm-metallic palettes
palette_no_overlap: the electric/ice blue of the head does not appear in any peer sprite — palette island
highlight_intensity_outlier: blue flame emits a near-glow brightness level that exceeds the modest specular highlights on all peer items
proportion_tall: candidate height-to-width ratio is visibly more elongated than the compact, stocky peers; handle reads overly long relative to head mass
charm_level_drift: peers read earthy/practical warrior gear; candidate reads magical/dramatic — tonal register slightly outside the cohort's cuteness band
reasoning: Rendering style and outline weight are on-model (1px dark outlines, consistent pixel density). Shadow direction is acceptable. Detail density is appropriate. However four issues compound into a meaningful style break: (1) The holy mace head is rendered in a saturated cold blue/white that has zero palette overlap with every peer item — peers are exclusively warm metallics (silvers, golds, red plumes, dark irons). (2) That blue element also carries higher luminance contrast than any peer highlight, functioning visually as a light emitter. (3) The sprite is noticeably taller and thinner than the reference set; peers cluster around squarish-to-moderate aspect ratios, the candidate is a slim vertical. (4) The 'cute echo' charm register the peers carry is slightly undercut by the dramatic magical torch-flame aesthetic. None of these are fatal alone, but together they prevent a clean pass.
